items.title: J&J glows with $1B buyout of Firefly and its degrader platform tech
items.snippet: The acquisition gives Johnson & Johnson access to Firefly Bio’s next-gen platform designed to create degrader antibody conjugates that can crack the tricky KRAS cancer target.

items.title: Fulcrum axes 85% of staff after walking away from lead asset
items.snippet: Follow along as BioSpace tracks job cuts and restructuring initiatives.

items.title: Incyte’s stars align as new CEO strikes $2B Vega takeover for blood disorder asset
items.snippet: Incyte is acquiring Vega Therapeutics, a subsidiary of Star Therapeutics, for a bleeding disorder program that analysts say has “pipeline-in-a-product” potential.

items.title: ‘WOW!’: RevMed and Tango’s pancreatic cancer combo achieves 92% response
items.snippet: After Revolution Medicine’s groundbreaking data drop in April, its Tango-partnered combination approach has demonstrated what analysts called “unprecedented” results for 12 patients with pancreatic cancer, teeing up a late-stage study.

items.title: Roche makes up to $3B blood cancer play with Nurix’s protein degrader
items.snippet: Roche and Nurix Therapeutics will advance their BTK degrader for chronic lymphocytic leukemia, as well as immunology and neurology indications.

items.title: ADA: Boehringer leans on fat mass benefits as obesity bet delivers ‘less competitive’ weight reduction
items.snippet: While survodutide’s 16.6% overall weight loss was underwhelming, Boehringer Ingelheim and Zealand Pharma’s drug achieved “impressive” fat loss, according to BMO Capital Markets.

items.title: ADA: Kailera ‘back at work’ after record-breaking IPO with China data in hand
items.snippet: After a $625 million IPO, the biggest ever in biotech, obesity-focused Kailera Therapeutics is readying a commercial strategy that puts patients at the center.

items.title: ADA: Metsera asset data support Pfizer's ‘entry point’ into obesity market
items.snippet: Much work needs to be done for Pfizer to be able to catch up to the weight-loss frontrunners, according to Guggenheim Partners, but new data from Metsera’s lead asset could set the pharma apart from competitors with a monthly injection.

items.title: ADA: Lilly sets ‘new record in Weight Loss Olympics’ with next-gen asset
items.snippet: Over two years of treatment, Eli Lilly’s triple-G drug cut body weight by more than 30% in certain patients with obesity, cementing the pharma’s position as the frontrunner in the metabolic space.

items.title: Busy FDA gives rare disease sector complementary pathways, unanswered questions
items.snippet: The FDA has expanded the regulatory toolkit during President Donald Trump’s second term, adding new mechanisms for rare diseases while putting the Biden-era platform technology designation into action.

items.title: Post-Makary, new rare disease framework faces fresh uncertainty
items.snippet: As the FDA tries to clarify its intent for former FDA Commissioner Marty Makary’s plausible mechanism framework for bespoke therapies, experts emphasize the importance of expanding its scope to encompass rare diseases that affect more than just one or a few individuals.

items.title: ADA: Lilly’s weight loss mastery shows benefits in menopause, sleep apnea, more
items.snippet: Eli Lilly sauntered into the American Diabetes Association meeting with a commanding lead in the metabolic space and put down more evidence for its pipeline, including new pill Foundayo and next-gen asset retatrutide, in new indications.

items.title: ADA: Obesity partners Zealand and Roche aim for weight loss ‘sweet spot’ with amylin agonist
items.snippet: Analysts and investors were unimpressed by Phase 2 data posted in the spring showing that an amylin analog developed by Roche and partner Zealand Pharma elicited 9% weight loss, less than Eli Lilly’s rival candidate. Executives from both companies told BioSpace that premium weight loss is not the point of petrelintide.

items.title: Lilly and Novo face off at ADA 2026 as others seek to compete in obesity
items.snippet: The American Diabetes Association's annual congress will feature a superstar lineup, including weight loss giants Eli Lilly and Novo Nordisk. But several scrappy biotechs will also present obesity candidates with the potential to match—if not outperform—their deep-pocketed competitors.

items.title: Fulcrum lays off 85% of staff after dropping asset, leaving 9 employees
items.snippet: After discontinuing development of its lead asset, Fulcrum Therapeutics has provided more details on its restructure, with 48 of 57 employees headed out the door.

items.title: The next FDA commissioner must be a champion for gene therapies
items.snippet: The FDA must provide consistent and predictable regulatory frameworks if the U.S. is to maintain its leadership in gene therapy, one of the most consequential therapeutic fields of our generation.

items.title: Otsuka energizes IgA neuropathy space with new Phase 3 Voyxact data
items.snippet: By inhibiting the APRIL cascade, Otsuka Pharmaceuticals’ Voyxact slowed kidney function decline in patients with IgAN, opening up a potential path for full approval while also reading through to Vertex and others with similar assets.

items.title: Stakeholders want Makary’s voucher program paused, call for FDA independence
items.snippet: The FDA hasn’t been transparent and open enough with how it has implemented the Commissioner’s National Priority Voucher program, patient, industry and trade groups said Thursday.

items.title: Surviving (and thriving with) DEI in biotech
items.snippet: Rather than getting hung up on what to call DEI in the workplace, leaders should take three specific actions to help their employees embrace and engage with it. Companies and the patients they serve will benefit.

items.title: Massachusetts’ life sciences employment dipped last year following 14 years of growth: report
items.snippet: Massachusetts’ life sciences jobs declined 1% in 2025, according to a new MassBioEd report. However, the report is projecting industry employment will increase 9.7% by 2030. It also noted reskilling needs for some roles, including scientists.

items.title: Lilly just bought renowned regulator Peter Marks a pipeline to play with
items.snippet: FDA veteran Peter Marks will now shape the future of Eli Lilly’s vaccines work after the buys of Curevo, LimmaTech Biologics and Vaccine Company for up to $3.8 billion total.

items.title: 3 top FDA, CDC roles filled by 15 different people in less than 18 months
items.snippet: The Department of Health and Human Services is spinning its wheels, unable to establish steady leadership at three major divisions—the CDC and the FDA’s two primary review units.

items.title: Unforced errors flummox HHS’ radical transparency bid—again
items.snippet: Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s health department has consistently touted radical transparency as being key to its mission. Recent instances—the FDA’s decision not to disclose the recipients of three Commissioner’s National Priority Vouchers and FDA and CDC choices not to publish vaccine-related papers—call this intent into q...

items.title: BioSpace elevates journalism as biopharma evolves in the Trump era
items.snippet: Neal and Azbee awards have validated our approach to reporting on the industry at a time of unprecedented shifts at the FDA and other federal agencies.
